# mediaPlayPauseButtonFrame

**Instance Property**

The frame rectangle to use for displaying a media playback button.

## Declaration

```swift
optional var mediaPlayPauseButtonFrame: CGRect { get }
```

## Discussion

If you support the playback of media directly from your notification interface, implement this property and use it to return a nonempty rectangle specified in the coordinate system of your view controller’s view. The system draws a button in the provided rectangle that lets the user play and pause your media content. The system handles the drawing of the button for you and calls the [doc://com.apple.User-Notifications-UI/documentation/UserNotificationsUI/UNNotificationContentExtension/mediaPlay()] and [doc://com.apple.User-Notifications-UI/documentation/UserNotificationsUI/UNNotificationContentExtension/mediaPause()] methods in response to user interactions. You can place this button anywhere in your view controller’s view.

If you don’t implement this property, the system doesn’t draw a media playback button.

## Supporting Media Playback

- **mediaPlayPauseButtonType**: The type of media button type to display.
- **UNNotificationContentExtensionMediaPlayPauseButtonType**: Constants indicating the type of media button to display.
- **mediaPlayPauseButtonTintColor**: The tint color for the media playback button.
- **mediaPlay()**: Tells you to begin playback of your media content.
- **mediaPause()**: Tells you to pause playback of your media content.
